contentBlocks.translation: Opening refrain: Sāmbasadāśiva! Sāmbasadāśiva! Sāmbasadāśiva! Sāmbaśiva! (Śiva with Ambā — the eternal auspicious one, ever blessed with Pārvatī!)
Letter A (अ): Adbhutavigraha (of wondrous form), Amarādhīśvara (lord of the immortals), Agaṇitaguṇagaṇa (of countless virtues), Amṛtaśiva (the nectar-Śiva) — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter Ā (आ): Ānandāmṛta (nectar of bliss), Āśritarakṣaka (protector of those who take refuge), Ātmānanda (bliss of the Self), Mahēśa, Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter I (इ): Indukaḷādhara (bearing the crescent moon), Indrādipriya (dear to Indra and the gods), Sundararūpa (of beautiful form), Surēśa, Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

contentBlocks.original: ईश सुरेश महेश जनप्रिय केशवसेवितपाद शिव ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: īśa surēśa mahēśa janapriya kēśavasēvitapāda śiva ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Letter Ī (ई): Īśa (the ruler), Surēśa (lord of the gods), Mahēśa (the great lord), Janapriya (dear to all people), Kēśavasēvitapāda (whose feet are served by Viṣṇu/Keśava), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter U (उ): Uragādipriyabhūṣaṇa (adorned with serpents as beloved ornaments), Śaṅkara (the auspicious maker), Narakavināśa (destroyer of hell), Naṭēśa (lord of the cosmic dance), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter Ū (ऊ): Ūrjitadānavanāśa (destroyer of the mightily arrogant demons), Parātpara (beyond the beyond), Ārjitapāpavināśa (destroyer of accumulated sins), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter Ṛ (ऋ): Ṛgvēdaśrutimauḷivibhūṣaṇa (adorned with the Ṛgveda as the crown of all scripture), Ravichandrāgnitrinētra (whose three eyes are the sun, moon, and fire), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter Ṝ (ॠ): Ṝpamanādi (beginningless in form), Prapañchavilakṣaṇa (transcending and distinct from the universe), Tāpanivāraṇa (remover of suffering), Tattva (the ultimate reality), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter Ḷ (ऌ): Liṅgasvarūpa (whose form is the Liṅga), Sarvabudhapriya (dear to all the wise), Maṅgaḷamūrti (of auspicious form), Mahēśa, Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter Ḹ (ॡ): Lūtādhīśvara (the great sovereign lord), Rūpapriyaśiva (the auspicious lover of beauty), Vēdāntapriyavēdya (known through and beloved of Vedānta), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter E (ए): Ēkānēkasvarūpa (simultaneously one and many in form), Viśvēśvara (lord of the universe), Yōgihṛdipriyavāsa (who lovingly dwells in the hearts of yogis), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

contentBlocks.original: ऐश्वर्याश्रय चिन्मय चिद्घन अच्युतानन्त महेश शिव ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: aiśvaryāśraya chinmaya chidghana achyutānanta mahēśa śiva ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Letter AI (ऐ): Aiśvaryāśraya (refuge of all sovereignty), Chinmaya (pure consciousness), Chidghana (mass of pure awareness), Achyutānanta (imperishable and infinite), Mahēśa, Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter O (ओ): Ōṅkārapriya (who loves Oṃkāra), Uragavibhūṣaṇa (adorned with serpents), Hrīṅkārādi (beginning with the sacred bīja Hrīm), Mahēśa, Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter AU (औ): Aurasalālita (cherished by his own sons — Skanda and Vināyaka), Antakanāśana (destroyer of Death/Yama), Gaurisamēta (ever united with Gaurī), Girīśa (lord of the mountain), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter AṂ / Anusvāra (अं): Ambaravāsa (dwelling in the sky and the ether), Chidambaranāyaka (lord of Chidambaram, the space of consciousness), Tumburunāradasēvya (served by the divine musicians Tumburu and Nārada), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter AḤ / Visarga (अः): Āhārapriya (who is pleased by sacred offerings), Ādigirīśvara (primordial lord of the mountain), Bhōgādipriya (who loves enjoyment and all divine pleasures), Pūrṇa (the complete), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter KA (क): Kamalākṣārchita (worshipped by the lotus-eyed Viṣṇu), Kailāsapriya (who loves Kailāsa), Karuṇāsāgara (ocean of compassion), Kānti (radiance and beauty), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter KHA (ख): Khaḍgaśūlamṛgaḍhakkādyāyudha (bearing sword, trident, deer, drum, and other weapons), Vikramarūpa (of heroic form), Viśvēśa (lord of the universe), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter GA (ग): Gaṅgāgirisutavallabha (beloved of Gaṅgā and Pārvatī, the mountain's daughter), Guṇahita (beneficent through noble qualities), Śaṅkara, Sarvajanēśa (lord of all people), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter GHA (घ): Ghātakabhañjana (breaker of those who destroy), Pātakanāśana (destroyer of sins), Gaurisamēta (ever united with Gaurī), Girīśa, Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter ṄA (ङ): Śrutimauḷivibhūṣaṇa (adorned with the Vedas as his crown), Vēdasvarūpa (whose very form is the Veda), Viśvēśa, Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter CHA (च): Chaṇḍavināśana (destroyer of the fierce and arrogant), Sakalajanapriya (dear to all people), Maṇḍalādhīśa (lord of the sacred circle/realm), Mahēśa, Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter CHA (छ): Chatrakirīṭasukuṇḍalaśōbhita (adorned with parasol, crown, and beautiful earrings), Putrapriya (fond of his son Skanda), Bhuvanēśa (lord of all the worlds), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter JA (ज): Janmajarāmṛtināśana (destroyer of birth, old age, and death), Kalmaṣarahita (free from all taint and sin), Tāpavināśa (destroyer of all threefold suffering), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter JHA (झ): Jhaṅkārāśraya (who finds support in the resonance of Oṃ), Bhṛṅgiriṭipriya (dear to Bhṛṅgiriṭi, the devoted sage), Ōṅkārēśa (lord of Oṃkāra), Mahēśa, Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter JÑA (ञ): Jñānājñānavināśaka (destroyer of both ignorance and the notion of dualistic knowledge), Nirmala (spotlessly pure), Dīnajanapriya (dear to the humble and the poor), Dīpta (brilliantly radiant), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter ṬA (ट): Ṭaṅkādyāyudhadhāraṇa (bearing the ṭaṅka-axe and other weapons), Satvara (swiftly responsive), Hrīṅkārādi (presiding over the Hrīm mantra and others), Surēśa, Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter ṬHA (ठ): Ṭhaṅkasvarūpa (whose form resonates as the primordial sound), Sahakārōttama (the supreme supporter), Vāgīśvara (lord of speech), Varadēśa (lord who grants boons), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter ḌA (ड): Ḍambavināśana (destroyer of pride and pretension), Ḍiṇḍimabhūṣaṇa (adorned with the ḍamaru drum), Ambaravāsa (sky-dweller), Chidīśa (lord of pure consciousness), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter ḌHA (ढ): Ḍhaṇḍhaṇḍamaruka (whose ḍamaru beats out the primordial rhythm of creation), Dharaṇīniśchala (firm and immovable as the earth), Ḍhuṇḍhivināyakasēvya (served by Ḍhuṇḍhi-Vināyaka), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter ṆA (ण): Naḷinavilōchana (lotus-eyed), Naṭanamanōhara (enchanting in his cosmic dance), Alikulabhūṣaṇa (adorned like a swarm of bees — the humming of sacred sound), Amṛta (the nectar of immortality), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter TA (त): Tattvamasītyādi-vākyasvarūpaka (whose form is the great Vedāntic statements 'Tat tvam asi' and the like), Nityānanda (eternal bliss), Mahēśa, Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter THA (थ — as in sthāvara): Sthāvarajaṅgamabhuvanavilakṣaṇa (transcending and distinct from all worlds of the moving and non-moving), Bhāvukamunivarasēvya (served by the best of inspired and devoted sages), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter DA (द): Duḥkhavināśana (destroyer of all suffering), Dalitamanōnmana (who has subdued the intoxication of the mind), Chandanalēpitacharaṇa (whose feet are anointed with sandalwood paste), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter DHA (ध): Dharaṇīdhara (bearer of the earth), Śubha (the auspicious), Dhavaḷavibhāsvara (brilliantly white and radiant), Dhanadādipriyadāna (who loves to bestow wealth and all good gifts), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter NA (न): Nānāmaṇigaṇabhūṣaṇa (adorned with many kinds of precious gems), Nirguṇa (beyond all qualities and attributes), Naṭanajanasupriyanāṭya (whose cosmic dance is dearly loved by all who witness it), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter PA (प): Pannagabhūṣaṇa (adorned with serpents), Pārvatināyaka (lord of Pārvatī), Paramānanda (supreme bliss), Parēśa (the transcendent Lord), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter PHA (फ): Phālavilōchana (with the third eye on the forehead), Bhānukōṭiprabha (radiant as ten million suns), Hālāhaladhara (bearer of the world-consuming Hālāhala poison), Amṛta (the immortal nectar), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter BA (ब): Bandhavināśana (destroyer of all bondage), Bṛhadīśāmaraskandādipriya (dear to Bṛhaspati, Indra, Skanda, and other great ones), Kanaka (golden), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter BHA (भ): Bhasmavilēpana (anointed with sacred ash), Bhavabhayanāśana (destroyer of the fear of worldly existence), Vismayarūpa (of wondrous form), Viśvēśa, Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter MA (म): Manmathanāśana (destroyer of Manmatha/Kāma, the god of love), Madhupānapriya (who loves the sweet drink of nectar), Mandaraparvatavāsa (who dwells on Mount Mandara), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter YA (य): Yatijanahṛdayanivāsita (who dwells in the hearts of renunciants and devoted seekers), Īśvara, Vidhiviṣṇvādi-surēśa (lord of gods beginning with Brahmā and Viṣṇu), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter RA (र): Rāmēśvara (lord of all that is lovely), Ramaṇīyamukhāmbuja (with a beautiful lotus face), Sōmēśvara (lord of the moon), Sukṛtēśa (lord of the meritorious), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter LA (ल): Laṅkādhīśvara (supreme sovereign), Suragaṇasēvita (served by all the hosts of gods), Lāvaṇyāmṛtalasita (shining with the nectar of beauty), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter VA (व): Varadābhayakara (granting boons and fearlessness with his hands), Vāsukibhūṣaṇa (adorned with the great serpent Vāsuki), Vanamālādivibhūṣa (adorned with garlands of forest flowers and other ornaments), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

contentBlocks.original: शान्तिस्वरूप जगत्त्रय चिन्मय कान्तिमतीप्रिय कनक शिव ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: śāntisvarūpa jagattraya chinmaya kāntimatīpriya kanaka śiva ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Letter ŚA (श): Śāntisvarūpa (whose very form is peace), Jagattraya (the three worlds in their entirety), Chinmaya (pure consciousness pervading all), Kāntimatīpriya (dear to the luminous, beloved by the gracious), Kanaka (golden), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter ṢA (ष): Ṣaṇmukhajanaka (father of Ṣaṇmukha/Skanda), Surēndramunipriya (dear to Indra and to the sages), Ṣāḍguṇyādisamēta (endowed with all six divine qualities and more), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!
Letter SA (स): Saṃsārārṇavanāśana (destroyer of the ocean of worldly existence), Śāśvatasādhuhṛdipriyavāsa (who lovingly and eternally dwells in the hearts of true saints), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter HA (ह): Hara (the remover of all that binds), Puruṣōttama (the supreme Person), Advaitāmṛtapūrṇa (full of the nectar of non-duality), Murārisusēvya (well served by Murāri/Viṣṇu), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter ḶA (ळ): Ḷāḷitabhaktajanēśa (lord who tenderly nurtures his devotees), Nijēśvara (the self-sovereign, intrinsic lord), Kāḷinaṭēśvara (lord of Kāḷī's dance), Kāma (love and bliss itself), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Letter KṢA (क्ष): Kṣararūpādipriyānvita (present in and beloved of both the perishable and imperishable forms), Sundara (the beautiful), Sākṣijagattraya (witness of all three worlds), Svāmi (the Lord), Śiva — Sāmbasadāśiva!

Closing refrain: Sāmbasadāśiva! Sāmbasadāśiva! Sāmbasadāśiva! Sāmbaśiva! (Having traversed all letters of the alphabet, the sacred name resounds: Śiva, ever united with Ambā, the eternal auspicious one!)
Thus ends the Śrī Sāmbasadāśiva Mātṛkāvarṇamālikā Stotram (the Garland of All Letters in Praise of Sāmbasadāśiva).
